The Data Disconnect Managing a network of 150+ wholesalers and 150-quintal lorry loads shouldn't require being chained to a desk. The real bottleneck wasn't the logistics; it was the data availability. Whenever my father was in the field without his laptop, critical operations stalled. There was no instant access to GSTINs, no way to quickly verify addresses, and manually creating mill-standard Excel sheets to send via Gmail from a phone was a fragmented, time-consuming nightmare.

? ??? ????????? ????:
 An AI PM?s Approach Using frameworks like Antigravity and Codex, I treated this as a professional product lifecycle, applying a systematic flow to engineer a way out of this bottleneck:

[ ? ] Discovery: Extracting the "Pure Engineering Soul" 
[ ? ] Requirements: Zero-friction offline specs 
[ ?? ] Architecture: Zero-downtime foundation 
[ ? ] MVP Execution: High-performance mobile UI 
[ ? ] Iterative R&D: Data hardening & cloud sync

?? ??? ????????:?
RiceAgent Pro I developed a mobile powerhouse to put the entire office into his pocket:

? Automated .xlsx: Visually aligned, mill-standard order exports.
? Resilient SQLite: Zero data loss with Drift local caching.
? Intelligent Pipeline: Raw data sanitization and format-bypassing.
? Omnichannel Routing: Instant WhatsApp and Email dispatch.

? ??????????? ??? ???????
By applying a Utility-First strategy, the business is no longer bound to a laptop. Operations are faster, cleaner, and always "one step ahead." Building RiceAgent reinforced my belief that the best software doesn't just execute code?it gives people their time back. I am driven to bring this same AI PM mindset?mixing rigorous research, rapid prototyping, and scalable architecture?to tackle broader, complex challenges in the industry.

Engineering is often taught as a process of technical optimization, but true innovation happens when we design for human realities. 

From May to July 2025, my work with the Amrita Vishwa Vidyapeetham, Coimbatore Live-in-Labs? program took me to Nediamanickam village in Ramanathapuram at Tamil Nadu. Tasked with addressing rural water sustainability, the experience quickly evolved into a profound lesson in systems-level thinking and co-design.

Through Human-Centered Design (HCD) and ????????????? ????? ????????? (???), my team and I didn?t just observe from the outside; we immersed ourselves. The reality on the ground was stark : families navigating the fallout of abandoned infrastructure and spending a massive portion of their income - up to ?2160 monthly-on private water vendors. The core issue wasn't just technical; it was a systemic challenge intertwining governance, livelihood, and community trust.

O?u?t?c?o?m?e?s? ?&? ?I?m?p?a?c?t?:?
? ???????????? ??????????????: A major milestone of this journey was implementing Amrita?s community-scale water filtration initiative. We didn't just install a system; we built a sustainable management system for protection, purification, and management. By focusing on the Oorani (traditional water body) as a primary source, we bridged the gap between traditional wisdom and modern engineering, ensuring the solution was something the community could actually afford, maintain, and own.
? ???? ????????????: This work is now evolving into an academic contribution. My team and I are currently working on IEEE publications to document our findings on rural sustainability, HCD-PRA integration, and community-led engineering models.

M?y? ?L?e?a?r?n?i?n?g?s?:?
? ?????? ?????? ??? ?????: Utilizing PRA tools like transect walks and resource mapping ensures solutions are grounded in actual human needs.
? ??????? ???? ????????: Solving complex problems requires looking beyond technical specs to the intersecting realities of health, economics, and culture.
? ??????????? ???? ???????: Meaningful technology isn't just about the most advanced system; it?s about creating a sustainable management ecosystem that empowers people.

This experience represents a steady, linear progression in my journey as a researcher and developer. It has reinforced my commitment to a "smart work" approach?focusing on high-leverage root causes, interdisciplinary thinking, and building technology that leaves a tangible, positive impact.
